stem: A line intersects two parallel lines, forming four acute angles and four obtuse angles. The measure of one of these eight angles is (9x − 300)°. The sum of the measures of four of the eight angles is k°. Which of the following could NOT be equivalent to k, for all values of x?
rationale: All four acute angles are equal in measure, and all four obtuse angles are equal in measure (since the lines are parallel). With acute = (9x − 300)°, obtuse = (480 − 9x)°. The possible sums of any 4 of the 8 angles are: 4 acute = 36x − 1200; 4 obtuse = 1920 − 36x; 2 acute + 2 obtuse = 360; 3 acute + 1 obtuse = 27x − 900 + 480 − 9x = 18x − 420; 1 acute + 3 obtuse = 9x − 300 + 1440 − 27x = −18x + 1140. The expression −18x + 1500 is not among these and so cannot equal the sum of any 4 of these angles for all x.
